series_description:
Audits and reviews performed by an
agency on external entities, on the agency
by other entities, or on the agency by
internal auditors. Includes working papers
that support the audit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
Note: The State Auditor’s Office
(SAO) retains any copies of its
audits performed on Texas State
agencies. See Agency Item # (AIN)
TPW 5.4.018 and TPW 5.4.019 for
Audit Plan records.

series_description:
Including any supporting documentation
created and/or used to justify and support
legislative appropriations requests by an
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
Note: Report is developed and
electronically distributed by
Financial Resources.
Archives Note: The final version of
Legislative Appropriation Requests
must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(3)(A).
Working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final plan are not subject to archival
review and may be disposed of at
the expiration of the retention
period.

series_description:
Complaints and other unsolicited feedback
received by an agency from the public
concerning the agency and records
pertaining to the resolution of complaints.
Does not include similar Law Enforcement
activities.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: If a complaint becomes
the subject of litigation, it must be
included in and is subject to the
minimum retention period of AIN
TPW 1.1.048.

series_description:
Incoming/outgoing and internal
correspondence, in any format, pertaining
to the formulation, planning,
implementation, interpretation,
modification, or redefinition of the
programs, services, or projects of an
agency and the administrative regulations,
policies, and procedures that govern them.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 4 years
comments:
CAUTION: This records series and
AIN TPW 1.1.008 should be used
only for correspondence that is not
included in or directly related to
another records series on the
agency's approved schedule.
Related Note: Correspondence
incorporated within another record
series must be retained as long as
the designated retention period for
that series. Note: Only the
administrative correspondence of
executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors and program heads
require archival review. Also see:
AIN TPW 1.1.007.B, 1.1.007.C and
1.1.007.D.

series_description: See 1.1.007.A description
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 4 years
comments:
Archives Note: The archival
requirement is met by sending the
records to the TPW Records
Management Branch for review,
then submission to the State
Archivist, Archives & Info Services
Div., TSLAC.

series_description: See 1.1.007.A description
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 4 years
comments: R = Review by State Archivist.

series_description: See 1.1.007.A description
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 4 years
comments: R = Review by State Archivist.

series_description:
Non-administrative incoming/outgoing and
internal correspondence, in any media
including electronic communication,
pertaining to or arising from the routine
operations of the policies, programs,
services, or projects of an agency.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 2 years
comments:
See Caution note for AIN TPW
1.1.007.A

series_description:
Any document that officially initiates,
rescinds, or amends general office
procedures.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Any document that initiates, rescinds, or
amends a regulation, policy, or procedure
that governs the programs, services, or
projects of an agency. Includes
Proclamations.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years
comments:
Note: The archival requirement is
met by sending the records to the
TPW Records Management Branch
for submission to the State
Archivist, Archives & Info Services
Div., TSLAC.

series_description:
A descriptive narrative that summarizes
Executive Order documents. The summary
restates the purpose of the document and
highlights the major points, results,
conclusions, and/or recommendations.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year
comments:
Archives Note: E= Archival review
code removed subsequent to
appraisal by the Archives and
Information Services Division,
TSLAC, 10/18/99.

series_description:
Calendars, appointment books, or
programs, and scheduling or itinerary
records, maintained by staff during
business hours that document
appointments, itineraries, and other
activities of an agency official or employee.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 1 year
comments:
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchased with personal funds but
used by a state official or employee
to document his or her work
activities may be a state record and
subject to this retention period. See
Open Records Decision 635 issued
in December 1995 by the Attorney
General. Archives
Note: Only the calendars,
appointment, and itinerary records
of elected officials, agency heads,
and board or commission members
require archival review. Contact the
State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.

series_description:
From agency legal counsel or the Attorney
General, including any requests eliciting
the opinions.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include legal
opinions or advice rendered on a
matter in litigation or with regard to
pending litigation or opinions
rendered for Public Information Act
Request. See AIN TPW 1.1.048,
1.1.020 and 1.1.021.
Archives Note: Opinions and advice
that set legal precedent or exhibit
historical value will be evaluated by
the State Archivist with TSLAC for
archival preservation. See page ix of
the State Records Retention
Schedule issued by TSLAC for
additional guidelines.
Note: The archival requirement is
met by sending the records to the
TPW Records Management Branch
for submission to the State
Archivist, Archives & Info Services
Div., TSLAC.

series_title:
Public Relations Records -
News or Press
series_description:
News, press releases, marketing files and
collateral, or any public relations files
maintained or issued by an agency.
Includes print, electronic, audio (podcast),
and audiovisual records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Note: The archival requirement is
met by sending the records to the
TPW Records Management Branch
for submission to the State
Archivist, Archives & Info Services
Div., TSLAC.

series_title:
Public Relations Records -
Radio Programs Only
series_description:
Passport to Texas and outreach
programming aired on radio stations;
records can include mixed media and
support documentation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Archives Note: E = 10/18/99
Archival review code removed
subsequent to appraisal by the
Archives & Info. Services Div.,
TSLAC.

series_title:
Public Information
Requests (Open Records
Requests) - Not
Exception/Fulfilled
series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are furnished to the public
under the Public Information Act (Chapter
552, Government Code). Also includes
withdrawn requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_title:
Public Information
Requests (Open Records
Requests) - Exception/Not
fulfilled
series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are excepted/not fullfilled
under the Public Information Act (Chapter
552, Government Code), including records
provided to the legislature under section
552.008.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Charts or diagrams that show the structure
of an agency and the relationships and
relative ranks of its departments and job
positions.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
Archival Note: Only charts showing
the overall arrangement and
administrative structure of the state
agency need to be transferred to
the State Archives with TSLAC.
Organizational charts showing
division/department level detail are
not considered archival.

series_description:
Plans and records relating to the process
of planning new or redefined programs,
services or projects of an agency that are
not included in or directly related to other
records series in this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
Archives Note: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_description: See 1.1.024.A for description
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
Archives Note: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_description: See 1.1.024.A for description
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
Archives Note: Data processing

comments:

series_description: See 1.1.024.A for description
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
Archives Note: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_title:
Plans and Planning
Records-Other Divisions
not listed above
series_description: See 1.1.024.A for description
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
Archives Note: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_description:
Copies of all proposed, withdrawn,
emergency, and adopted rules; open
meetings notices; or any other documents
required by law to be submitted to the
Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Drafts of proposed legislation and related
correspondence.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Surveys soliciting feedback from
customers, clients, or stakeholders of an
agency, and the statistical data maintained
rating an agency's performance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
Note: See AIN TPW 1.1.067 for
summary reports compiled from
customer surveys.

series_description:
Notes or text of speeches, papers, or
presentations delivered in conjunction
with agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
Archives Note: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given by
or on behalf of executive staff,
board or commission members,
division directors and program
heads require archival review. Note:
The archival requirement is met by
sending the records to the TPW
Records Management Branch for
submission to the State Archivist,
Archives & Info Services Div., TSLAC.
Note: See AIN TPW 1.1.043

series_description:
Instructional materials and other records
developed by an agency associated with
training the entities or non-personnel
individuals it regulates or serves, including
but not limited to training manuals, course
registration, class rosters, sign-in sheets,
syllabuses, course outlines, and similar
training aids used in external training
programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
hazardous material training records.
See AIN TPW 5.4.007. Note: See AIN
TPW 3.3.030 for internal personnel
training materials.

series_description:
Records created by or on behalf of an
agency in anticipation of or in the
adjudication of a lawsuit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years
comments:
Archives Note: Cases that set legal
precedent or exhibit historical value
will be evaluated by the Archives
and Information Services Division of
the Texas State Library and Archives
Commission for archival
preservation. See page ix of the
State Records Retention Schedule
issued by TSLAC for additional
guidelines.

series_description:
Logs or similar records used to register
persons appearing before state agencies as
required by Chapter 2004, Government
Code, including quarterly reports filed with
the Texas Ethics Commission (TEC).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Operational strategic plans, which include
descriptions of information resources
programs, prepared in accordance with
Government Code, 2054.095 and
2056.002. Includes working files and
related documentation used in creating
the final plan.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
Note: Governement Code 2056.002
requirement is met by sending the
required 2 copies to TPW Records
Management Branch for submission
to the Texas State Publications
Depository Program, Archives &
Info. Services Div., TSLAC.

series_description:
Self-evaluations and plans documenting
compliance with the requirements of the
Americans with Disabilities Act.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Agendas and minutes of state boards,
committees, commissions, and councils
that conduct open meetings as required by
Chapter 551, Government Code.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
CAUTION: This records series and
AIN TPW 1.1.059, 1.1.060, 1.1.061,
and 1.1.062 must be used for those
state boards, committees,
commissions, and councils, which
by law or the biennial
Appropriations Act, are
administered by another state
agency. These records and all
others related to the functions of
any of these dependent entities
must be included in the records
retention schedule of the
administering agency. Archives
Note: Agency retains permanent
record copy. Archival requirement
is met by sending one microfilm
copy to Archives and Information
Services Division, TSLAC.

series_title:
Meetings, Agendas and
Minutes or Audiovisual
Recordings of Closed
Meetings
series_description:
Agendas, minutes, or audiovisual
recordings of closed meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
Note: See caution comment at AIN
TPW 1.1.058.

series_title:
Meetings, Audiovisual
Recordings of Open
Meetings
series_description:
Audiovisual recordings of open meetings
of state boards, commissions, committees,
and councils.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 90 days
comments:
CAUTION: It is an exception to the
AC+90 days retention that if an
agency does not prepare written
minutes, it must retain recordings
of open meetings permanently,
following the same retention
requirements of AIN TPW 1.1.058.

series_description:
Notes taken during open meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils from which written minutes are
prepared.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 90 days
comments:
Note: See caution comment at AIN
TPW 1.1.058.

series_description:
Minutes or notes, and supporting
documentation, taken at internal agency
staff meetings.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 1 year

series_description:
Any records of an agency needed for the
documentation of output, outcome,
efficiency, and explanatory measures in an
agency's appropriations request or
strategic plan, and for performance
measures used to manage the agency.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: The FISCAL YEAR END + 3
retention period overrides any
shorter retention period for a
records series in this schedule if the
records series is needed for
documentation of agency
performance measures.
Note: See AIN TPW 1.1.068 for
reports on agency performance
measures.
Note: When Creating A New Record
-- The publication requirement is
met by sending the required 2
copies to TPW Records
Management Branch for submission
to the Texas State Publications
Depository Program, Archives &
Info. Services Div., TSLAC.

series_title:
Reports and Studies (Non-
Fiscal) - Raw Data
series_description:
Information or data collected and
compiled for the purpose of producing non-
fiscal reports.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include source
documentation or used for
information or data included in or
directly related to another records
series in this schedule. See AIN TPW
1.1.064. Note:
When using data or information to
report to regulating agency, defer
to the records retention process of
that authority.

series_description:
Includes but not limited to these
collections: Geographic Information
Systems, Archeological Sites including
caves.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
Notes: When Creating New Records
-- The publication and archival
requirements are met by sending
the required 5 paper copies to TPW
Records Management Branch for
submission to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program,
Archives & Info. Services Div., TSLAC
and Archival requirements would be
met if digital records are sent to the
Texas Digital Archives, TSLAC.
Old Paper Records OR Items Not
Sent When Created -- The archival
requirement is met by sending the
“original” paper records yearly
(Sept.1), after they have fulfilled
their retention, to the TPW Records
Management Branch, for
submission to the State Archivist,
Archives & Info. Services Div.,
TSLAC.

series_description:
Biennial narrative reports to the governor
and legislature as required by an agency's
enabling statutes, including annual
narrative reports if they are required by
statute. Includes working files and related
documentation used in creating final
report.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
Archives Note: The final version of
these agency reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3. The requirement is
met by sending the required copies
of the reports to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program,
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission. Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final report may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_title:
Reports and Studies (Non-
Fiscal) State Parks
series_description:
Annual, sub-annual, or special reports or
studies on non-fiscal aspects of an
agency's programs, services, or projects
compiled by agency personnel, by advisory
committees, or by consultants under
contract with an agency that are not noted
elsewhere in this schedule. Includes
reports distributed either internally or to
other entities.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Archives Notes: Reports are archival
when they deal with a significant
aspect of the agency’s programs.
Note: The publication and archival
requirements are met by sending
the required copies to TPW Records
Management Branch for submission
to the Texas State Publications
Depository Program, Archives &
Info. Services Div., TSLAC.
Note: see AIN TPW 1.1.065 for raw
data used to produce reports.

series_description: See 1.1.067.A for description
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
See Archives Notes for AIN TPW
1.1.067.A.

series_title:
Reports and Studies (Non-
Fiscal) Inland Fisheries
series_description: See 1.1.067.A for description
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
See Archives Notes for AIN TPW
1.1.067.A.

series_title:
Reports and Studies (Non-
Fiscal) Coastal Fisheries
series_description: See 1.1.067.A for description
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
See Archives Notes for AIN TPW
1.1.067.A.

series_description: See 1.1.067.A for description
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
See Archives Notes for AIN TPW
1.1.067.A

series_title:
Reports and Studies (Non-
Fiscal) Law Enforcement
series_description: See 1.1.067.A for description
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
See Archives Notes for AIN TPW
1.1.067.A.

series_title:
Reports and Studies (Non-
Fiscal) Other Divisions not
listed above
series_description: See 1.1.067.A for description
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
See Archives Notes for AIN TPW
1.1.067.A.

series_title:
Reports and Studies (Non-
Fiscal) Legislative Bill
Analysis
series_description:
Documentation and reports compiled by
Executive Office that reflect agency
analysis on passed bills filled during
legislative session.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
Note: Records Management (RM)
Branch needs one paper copy of
passed bills analysis
documentation. RM will retain
paper record for retention period
but microfilm and transfer to
Archives & Info. Services Div.,
TSLAC.

series_description:
Quarterly and annual reports on agency
performance measures submitted to
executive and legislative offices.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
Note to Financial Resouces Div. (FR)
Planning, Analysis & Reporting:
Send electronic file of publication to
the Records Management Branch
for submission to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program,
TSLAC.
Note: See AIN TPW 1.1.064 for
documentation used to produce
reports on agency performance
measures.

series_description:
Reports compiled by agency personnel on
a daily or other periodic basis pertaining to
workload monitoring, task completion
times, number of public contacts, and
similar activities.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 1 year
comments:
CAUTION: If reports are used to
document performance measures,
see AIN TPW 1.1.064.

series_title:
Reports - Activity - Law
Enforcement Incident
Reports, Case Files
series_description:
Case Files and Incident reports recorded by
agency Law Enforcement or Park Police
Officers.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
CAUTION: If a case becomes the
subject of litigation, it must be
included in and is subject to the
minimum retention period of AIN
TPW 1.1.048.
Note: may include documentation
and data entered into incident
reporting system or databases.

series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules,
or similar records distributed internally for
the use of employees or externally to the
public or those individuals or entities
regulated by an agency that sets out the
rules, policies, and procedures that govern
an agency's programs, services, or
projects. Includes working files and related
documentation used in creating rules,
policies, and procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
Archives Note: Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final rules, policies, and
procedures are not subject to
archival requirement and may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_description:
Reports made to the Office of Attorney
General (OAG) on an agency's Public
Information Act activities, per Government
Code, 552.010.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 2 years

series_description:
Transcripts and final decisions of hearings
conducted as part of the regulatory
process, and hearings on proposed rules
and changes.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
Archives Note: Only records that
lead to substantial or statutory
changes to the operations of the
agency and its policies and
procedures should be retained for
archival review. Contact the State
Archives when these records have
met their retention periods.
CAUTION: These records may be
maintained with related
information, including meeting
notices, proofs of publication, and
meeting minutes, and require a
longer retention period.

series_description:
Sunset Review Report, agency self-study,
and other correspondence and supporting
documentation related to the Sunset
review process for state agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
Archives Note: Related
documentation includes the agency
self-evaluation report, agency
response to the commission's draft
report, and documentation of the
agency's implementation of the
commission's final
recommendations.

series_description:
Final agreement described by Government
Code, 2009.054(c), associated with a
matter conducted under an alternative
dispute resolution procedure in which
personnel of a state agency participated as
a party on the agency's behalf.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
legal_citation:
Texas Civil Practice
and Remedies Code,
Chapter 154.071.
Government Code,
2009.054.

series_description:
Subpoenas for production of evidence
produced for litigation in which the state
agency is not a party. Includes legal
documents requiring recipient to appear in
court to testify, or to produce records to
be used in litigation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
Note: For subpoenas related to
litigation in which the state agency
is a party, see AIN TPW 1.1.048.

series_description:
Records that document the release of
records or information through any
method other than a Public Information
Act request or subpoena (including
employment verification).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Some records releases
may require a longer retention
period. Agencies must determine if
a longer retention period is
required based on any federal or
state statutes or regulations that
apply to the agency's functions.
Agency legal staff should be
consulted.
Note: See AIN TPW 1.1.020 for
records released under the Public
Information Act. See AIN TPW
1.1.076 for records produced for a
subpoena. See AIN TPW 1.1.048 for
records produced for litigation.

series_title:
Waivers of Liability/
General or Specific Liability
Release
series_description:
Waivers of liability, including statements
signed by volunteers acknowledging non-
entitlement to benefits, agreeing to abide
by state agency policies, etc. Signed or
acknowledged forms regarding park
activities, group use area or other use
activities, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
CAUTION: If an accident occurs to
any person covered by a signed
waiver of liability, it must be
retained for the same period as
accident reports. See AIN TPW
5.4.001 and 5.4.014a/b. Note: See
AIN TPW TPW 3.3.TPW1 and TPW
3.3.TPW2 for Volunteer records.
Note: Replaces TPW 5.4.TPW5;
Liability Release, from 2018 Agency
Retention Schedule.

series_description:
Copyright records which pertain to
employees’ original work. May include but
not limited to a copy of the work itself as
submitted to the United States Copyright
Office and the corresponding copyright
application, registration notice, and
supplementary documents.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Collections of related documents kept in
name or number order, etc., reflecting
research, services, agreements, or relating
to specific objectives and requires long
term relation and/or kept for research use.
Does not include Construction Projects.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Note: See AIN TPW 5.2.002 for
Construction Projects.

series_description: See TPW 1.1.TPW2.A for description
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: See TPW 1.1.TPW2.A for description
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: See TPW 1.1.TPW2.A for description
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: See TPW 1.1.TPW2.A for description
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: See TPW 1.1.TPW2.A for description
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Project/Program Files,
Other Divisions not listed
above
series_description: See TPW 1.1.TPW2.A for description
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Documents authorizing final disposition of
records under a certified records retention
schedule. Records may also include
destruction authorizations (e.g. form RMD
102) approved by Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: If destruction
authorizations are maintained as
part of AIN TPW 1.2.010 (Records
Disposition Logs), then longer
retention period applies. Note:
Destruction Authorizations and
Records Dispostion Logs are
currently combined through the
PWD1037 Form.

series_description:
Master versions of all forms used internally
and externally by the agency, including
subsequent revisions to an agency form or
any associated design or design
modification requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
A records retention schedule (i.e. form SLR
105) that identifies the records that are
created and maintained by an agency,
provides the minimum timeframes the
records must be retained, and includes
instructions for their disposition. This
series may include working files and
documentation of certification and
approval by the Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
Note: Original is retained by the
State and Local Records
Management Div/TSLAC; TPWD will
have a working convenience copy.

series_description:
Forms used to track the transmittal of
records to/from onsite and offsite storage
areas or a transfer of physical custody.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Logs or similar records listing records
disposed of by an agency, which might
include records series title, dates of
records, and date of disposition.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 10 years
comments:
CAUTION: Disposition can mean
destroyed or transferred.
Note: If destruction authorizations
are maintained as part of AIN TPW
1.2.010 (Records Disposition Logs),
then longer retention period
applies.
Note: Destruction Authorizations
and Records Dispostion Logs are
currently combined through the
PWD1037 Form.

series_description:
Worksheets or working papers used to
capture records inventory information,
including location tracking and to
document existence and requirements of a
records series.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Documentation used to provide and
improve access to records. Includes
indexes, card files, shelf lists, registers,
guides, and other finding aids.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: These records must carry
the same retention period and
archival code of the records they
support.

series_description:
Records management plans and similar
records that establish the policies and
procedures under which records and
information are managed in an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Information in any format that is publicly
distributed and produced by the authority
of or at the total or partial expense of a
state agency or is required to be
distributed under law by the agency.
except a publication that is subject to a
different retention period in this schedule.
Includes but is not limited to: a publication
distributed in print; on microform; as
audiovisual material; as interactive media
or on electronic external storage device; as
an online publication (including websites);
which is an index to other online
publications; as one or more text, graphic,
or other digital files; or as a user interface
to a computer database. Reteniton applies
to a master copy of produced publications
except a publication that is subject to a
different retention period in this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
CAUTION: Many state publications
must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission, by law. The
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission will retain a copy of all
state publications submitted to it
on a continuing basis, subject to
periodic evaluation to determine if
the publication merits further
retention.
Note: Send required number copies
of publications to Records
Management for submission to the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program, TSLAC.

series_description:
Specific to Texas Parks and Wildlife
Television Production episodes and stand
alone media productions broadcasted.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Note: The archival requirement is
met by sending one DVD copy of
each complete program after the
program aired, to the TPWD
Records Management Branch, for
submission to the State Archivist,
Archives & Info. Services Div.,
TSLAC.

series_description:
Physical and digital development and
design files used to create State
Publications, including but not limited to
background material, copy (drafts), original
artwork, photo negatives, prints, flats, etc.
This includes all work performed both
inside and outside the agency.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
Archives Note: Successive and
substantive drafts of major
publications may be archival. Major
is defined by both the publication's
authorship and its impact on Texas
and Texans. Original artwork,
including photo prints and
negatives that have significant value
as evidence of agency programs as
well as the potential for re-use, may
be archival.
Note: See AIN TPW 1.3.001 for final
State Publications created from
development files.

series_description:
Relatively long-lived computer files
containing organized and consistent sets of
complete and accurate electronic records
and associated processing files. Examples
include, but are not limited to, data tables
of relational databases used by
applications or computer programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Owners and
administrators of the data must be
certain before assigning the
retention period of AC to agency
master files and application data
that the files do not fall under
records series listed elsewhere.
Note: The disposal of monitoring
records that are automatically
overwritten need not be
documented through destruction
authorizations (AIN TPW 1.2.001) or
in records disposition logs (AIN TPW
1.2.010), but agency divisions
should consult with Records
Management and refer to Agency
RM policy in regard to disposition
of these records.

series_description:
Agency-developed automated software
applications, operating system files, and
associated processing files, including job
control language, programs, applications,
scripts, source code, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Records documenting operational and
maintenance requirements of computer
hardware such as operating manuals,
hardware/operating system requirements,
hardware configurations, equipment
control systems, and associated processing
files.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Records adequate to specify all technical
characteristics necessary for reading or
processing electronic records; to
document modifications to computer
programs; to access, retrieve, manipulate,
and interpret data in an automated
system; and allow for their timely,
authorized disposition. Records include but
are not limited to: user guides, system or
sub-system definitions, system
specifications, input and output
specifications, system flow charts, program
flow charts, program maintenance logs,
change notices, data element dictionaries,
file layouts, code books or tables, and
other records that explain the meaning,
purpose, structure, logical relationships,
and origin of the data elements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Files needed for electronic data audits.
Records include but are not limited to:
reports showing transactions accepted,
rejected, suspended, and/or processed;
history files or tapes; records of updates to
application files; and security logs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Hardware and software components for
monitoring agency’s computer system
resources and performance.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_title:
Chargeback Records to
Data Processing Services
Users
series_description:
Records documenting usage, costs, billing,
cost recovery, budgeting, and
administrative functions of computer
usage and data processing services for
individual units/departments/divisions in
an agency.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, or similar documents
establishing data processing policies and
procedures in an agency in such areas as
access and security, systems development,
data retention and disposition, data
ownership, production control, system
back-up, etc.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
technical documentation of
procedures necessary for reading or
processing of electronic records.
See AIN TPW 2.1.009.

series_description:
Forms and logs used to enter and reconcile
data sets submitted for processing.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Information verifying the quality of system,
hardware, or software operations. Records
include but are not limited to: records of
errors or failures and the loss of data
resulting from such failures;
documentation of abnormal termination
and of error-free processing; checks of
changes put into production; transaction
histories; and other records needed as an
audit trail to evaluate data accuracy.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
Note: For quality control records
related to non-IT procedures, see
AIN TPW 5.2.018.

series_description:
A record of web pages visited during an
internet session, including data files of
user-specific information created by the
webserver, that allows users to access
previously visited pages more quickly or to
generate a record of usage of a state-
owned computer.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
Note: The disposal of internet
history records need not be
documented through destruction
authorizations (AIN TPW 1.2.001) or
in records disposition logs (AIN TPW
1.2.010), but agency divisions
should consult with TPW Records
Management Branch and refer to
Agency RM policy in regard to
disposition of these records.

series_title:
Software Registrations,
Warranties and License
Agreements
series_description:
Records documenting the registration and
licensing of a software application to
activate the software for legal use by the
end users of a state agency, along with
warranties providing that the software will
perform in accordance with functional
specifications.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting the request for and
response to help desk tickets received by
divisions or units, such as information
technology.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Biennial information security plan for
protecting the security of the agency’s
information.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents whose
submission by candidates for vacant
positions is required on the application
form, by application procedures, or in the
employment advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include ADA
Accommodation Requests. See AIN
TPW 3.1.042.

series_title:
Personnel Files Including
Applications for
Employment - Hired
series_description:
Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents whose
submission by candidates for vacant
positions is required on the application
form, by application procedures, or in the
employment advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Note: See Master Personnel File list
in accordance to HR Policy HR-03-
11.

series_description:
Notes, memoranda, or reports relating to
the counseling of an employee for work-
related, personal, or substance abuse
problems. Usually maintained at the
supervisorial level except in those agencies
with counseling staff.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Agency copies of information relating to
the selection by employees of life, health,
vision, dental, disability, and other types of
insurance or benefits offered by the State
of Texas to its employees.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: Documents that serve as
payroll deduction authorizations
must be maintained for the
retention period prescribed for AIN
TPW 3.2.001.

series_description:
Internal or external announcements or
advertisements of job openings,
promotions, training programs, or
opportunities for overtime.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
Note: may be included in AIN TPW
3.1.014.

series_description:
Includes a contract or agreement regarding
the terms and conditions of employment
of an individual and a contract with a
vendor for temporary staffing services.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Includes notes of interviews with
candidates; questions asked of applicants;
audio and video recordings of job
interviews; driving record and previous
injury checks; pre-employment physical
examinations; and all other records that
document the selection process, except for
those noted in Remarks.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include criminal
history checks; see AIN TPW
3.1.026. Does not include drug
screening test results; see AIN TPW
3.1.040a/b/c. Does not include pre-
employments skills tests; see AIN
TPW 3.3.027 and 3.3.028. Does not
include pre-employment polygraph
examinations; see AIN TPW 3.1.043.
Note: May be included with AIN
TPW 3.1.012.

series_description:
Records relating to the review of employee
grievances or complaints against personnel
policies, working conditions, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaints filed by an agency
employee with the Equal
Employment Office (EEO) of the U.
S. Department of Labor (DOL). See
AIN TPW 1.1.048.

series_description:
Job evaluations, performance appraisals,
or other similar documents used to
evaluate the performance of an employee.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 2 years

series_description:
Corrective actions are those actions which
do not affect pay, status, or tenure and are
imposed to correct or improve an
employee's job performance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
CAUTION: If, during the retention
period of this records series, any
part of this series is used to
document and support personnel
disciplinary action under AIN TPW
3.1.021, all documentation from
this series used to support
disciplinary action must be retained
for the minimum retention period
described by AIN TPW 3.1.021.

series_description:
Disciplinary actions are those actions
which may affect pay, status, or tenure.
They are imposed to discipline an
employee whose conduct is harmful to the
best interests of the state, the agency, or
the employee work force; and for failure to
improve performance or conduct following
imposition of corrective action. May
include cause for demotion, suspension, or
dismissal, and the reasons for failure to
give written notice of resignation. May also
be used to document evidence of
employee self-improvement efforts, as
well as favorable and unfavorable
communication.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Forms or similar records used to create or
change information concerning the records
of an employee, including pay grade,
position classification, employee number,
evaluation date, and termination of
employment.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 2 years

series_description:
Job descriptions, including all associated
task or skill statements, for positions in an
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Medical or physical examination reports or
certificates of employees for whom
periodic monitoring of health or fitness is
required.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include pre-
employment physical examinations.
See AIN TPW 3.1.014. Does not
include medical or physical
examinations for employees
exposed to hazardous materials.
See AIN TPW 5.4.016a/b.

series_description:
Criminal history record information on job
applicants or agency employees obtained
from the Department of Public Safety
(DPS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Certificates of completion, certifications,
licenses, transcripts, test scores, or similar
records documenting the training, testing,
certification, licensing, or continuing
education achievements of an employee.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Employment Eligibility,
Documentation or
Verification of
series_description: Federal reporting form (Form I-9).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Federal regulation
requires that INS 1-9 forms be
retained for 3 years from
date of hire or 1 year after
separation of the employee,
whichever later. Agencies
should make certain that the INS I-9
forms for employees who terminate
from an
agency less than 3 years from date
of hire are kept for the 3 year
retention period.

series_description:
Retention period applies if an agency
replies to the sender of a resume that it
will be kept on file should future job
openings occur.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 1 year
comments:
Note: See AIN TPW 3.1.014 for
resumes, whether solicited or
unsolicited, that are used in any
way in the employment selection
process.

series_description:
Summary of apprenticeship applicant
qualifications, evaluation basis for
selection or rejection, original applications,
job assignments, promotions, separations,
layoffs, terminations, compensation
records, conditions of work, time records,
hours of training provided (separate from
labor), minority status, and gender of all
selected and rejected applicants.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 5 years

series_description: Awards, incentives, tenure, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Form completed and signed by employee
or official, or former employee or official,
electing to keep home address, home
telephone number, social security number,
and family information open or
confidential under the Public Information
Act, Government Code 552.024.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
CAUTION: Most recent public
access election information must be
kept as long as the former
employee verification records. See
AIN TPW 3.3.011.

series_description:
Consultation records, notes, letters,
memos, emails, reports, and other
documentation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaint filed with EEOC (Equal
Employment Opportunity
Commission). If matter becomes a
grievance or the subject of
counseling or litigation, or
employee is subject to disciplinary
action, the records are subject to
retention in the appropriate records
series. See AIN TPW 1.1.048,
3.1.006, 3.1.018, 3.1.020, and
3.1.021.

series_title:
Employee Drug Test and
Screening Records -
Positive Results and
Calibration
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
legal_citation:
49 CFR 382.403 for
commercial motor
vehicle drivers.

Employee Drug Test and
Screening Records -
Collection Records
series_description:
Records related to the alcohol and
controlled substances collection process
(except calibration of evidential breath
testing devices).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
See AIN TPW 3.1.040a for
calibration documentation.

series_title:
Employee Drug Test and
Screening Records -
Negative Results
series_description:
Records of negative and canceled
controlled substances test results and
alcohol test results with a concentration of
less than 0.02.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Agreements between employee and
agency authorizing certain actions,
including acknowledgement forms,
telecommuting agreements,
outside/secondary employment
authorizations, or other documentation
that show proof of receipt and awareness
of and adherence to agency policies and
procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
Note: See AIN TPW 5.4.012 for
security access records.

series_description:
Employee or applicant requests for
reasonable accommodation under the ADA
(Americans with Disabilities Act).

Polygraph examinations and results that

employment selection process.

series_description:
Documentation used to start, modify, or
stop all voluntary or required deductions
from payroll, including garnishment or
other court-ordered attachments.

series_description:
Payroll records and registers documenting
employee earnings, wages, and pay. This
may include but is not limited to payroll
input records, summary statements,
payroll vouchers, payroll detail sheets, and
payroll history.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 4 years

series_description:
Includes 1099, W2, FICA, and other tax
records.

series_description:
Used to make increases or decreases to
employees' gross pay, FICA, retirement, or
in the computation of taxes.

series_description:
Employer's copy of "Employee’s
Withholding Exemption Certificate".

series_description:
Records defining the wage or salary rate
for each position in the agency expressed
in dollars, grades, or step numbers.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 2 years

series_description:
Records and documentation relating to
unemployment compensation claims,
including reimbursement of funds
disbursed by Texas Workforce Commission
(TWC).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Forms used to deposit employee’s
earnings into a specified personal account.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Records documenting the amount of
pension or deferred compensation earned
by individual employees.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Note: For instructions regarding the
determination of the closure of
accounts and for additional
information regarding the
retention, see the most current
edition of the Benefits Coordinator
Reference Manual issued by the
Employees Retirement System of
Texas.

series_description:
Affirmative action plans for regular
employees and apprenticeship programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Affirmative action plans for contractors
and subcontractors.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Reports providing statistical information
on labor force.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Minimum information needed to verify
employment. Includes: name; social
security number; exact dates of
employment; last known address; and
most recent public access authorization.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 75 years
comments:
Note: TPWD Records Management
Branch is currently receiving CAPPS
reports from HR and will microfilm
records to store at TPWD Records
Facility. The report will be
combined with AIN TPW 3.1.038.

series_description:
Records relating to review and monitoring
of job classifications within an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Work, duty, shift, crew, or case schedules,
rosters, or assignments.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Reports to the agency from TWC or its
predecessor pertaining to employees.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Requests and authorizations for travel;
participation in educational programs,
workshops, or college classes; or for other
work-related activities for which the
expenses of the employee are defrayed or
reimbursed.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Any internally distributed manuals,
guidelines, or similar records that define

concerning the personnel of an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Any documents detailing the procedural
duties and responsibilities of agency
positions on a position-by-position basis.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Any reports compiled by an agency on
aspects of personnel staffing, including
listings of all staff by program or name,
staff hired during a month, detailed listings
of employees within its organizational
structure, position vacancies, analyses of
turnover rates and seasonality of
employment, etc.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Aptitude, competency, or skills tests and
checklists required of job applicants or of
current personnel to qualify for promotion

CAUTION: One copy of each
different master test (different in
terms of either questions or
administration procedures) should
be retained for the period
indicated.

series_description:
Completed aptitude, competency, or skills
test papers and checklists of job applicants
or of current personnel taking a test to
qualify for promotion or transfer.

series_description:
Records documenting the planning,
development, implementation,
administration and evaluation of in-house
training programs, including but not
limited to training manuals, course
registration, class rosters, sign-in sheets,
syllabuses, course outlines, and similar
training aids used in in-house training
programs.

CAUTION: Does not include
hazardous material training records.
See AIN TPW 5.4.007. Note: See AIN
TPW 1.1.043 for external training
records. See AIN TPW 3.1.027 for
individual employee training
records.

Note: Replaces AIN TPW 1.1.043.B

schedule.

comments:
Note: Replaces AIN TPW 1.1.043.C
from 2018 records retention
schedule.

approval acknowledgements, personal
information, offense (if applicable),
assignments, training documentation,
evaluations, log of hours, time sheets, or

Community Service and

series_description:

and facilities within the jurisdiction of the
institution. This series may include but is

photographs; notes which indicate or
document what happened, when, and
where; and related documentation and
correspondence.

comments:
CAUTION: If grant monies are
received to assist with the response
or recovery, the retention
requirements for this series may
vary depending on the specific
funding agency. Note: If any
litigation, claim, audit or other
action involving the records begins
prior to the expiration of the three-
year period, the records must be
maintained until resolution of all
issues or until the end of the three-
year period, whichever is later.

Continuity of Operations Plans
(COOP).
